2606.14892.title: Relational Structural Causal Models
2606.14892.paper_summary: An artificial intelligence must have a model of its environment that is causal, supporting reasoning about interventions and counterfactuals, and also combinatorial, supporting generalization to unseen combinations of objects. In this work, we formally study when and how such a model can be learned. We develop relational structural causal models, extending structural causal models (Pearl 2009) to settings where objects and their relations vary. First, we show how answers to not only causal but also observational queries about unseen combinations of objects can not be identified without further assumptions. To enable such identification--including in the presence of unobserved confounding--we define relational causal graphs and derive symbolic identification criteria. Finally, we propose relational neural causal models, a provably correct approach that outperforms non-relational baselines on simulated traffic scenes with varying cars, signals, and pedestrians.
2606.14892.paper_summary_zh: 人工智慧必須擁有一個因果的環境模型，能支持對干預和反事實的推理，並且還必須是組合性的，支持對未見物體組合的泛化。在本研究中，我們正式研究這樣的模型何時以及如何可以被學習。我們發展了關聯結構因果模型，將結構因果模型（Pearl 2009）擴展到物體及其關係變化的情境中。首先，我們展示了對未見物體組合的因果和觀察性查詢的答案，若不進一步假設，則無法識別。為了實現這種識別——包括在存在未觀察的混淆情況下——我們定義了關聯因果圖並推導了符號識別標準。最後，我們提出了關聯神經因果模型，這是一種可證明正確的方法，在模擬的交通場景中，對於變化的汽車、信號和行人，表現超過非關聯基準。
2606.14892.author: Adiba Ejaz et.al.
